item.title: Goldman Sees More Two-Year Volatility, Calmer Long End on Warsh
item.description: Kevin Warsh’s remarks after the Federal Reserve’s first policy decision under his chairmanship will probably spark more volatility at the shorter end of the Treasury curve while calming price swings at the long end, according to Kay Haigh at Goldman Sachs Asset Management.

item.title: Partners-Backed Eolo in Talks With Apollo Over Private Debt
item.description: Italian internet provider Eolo SpA is in advanced talks with Apollo Global Management over a roughly €500 million ($574 million) private debt package to refinance its liabilities, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cuba Makes Market-Friendly Changes in the Face of Trump Pressure
item.description: Cuba’s leadership signed off on a sweeping list of 176 market-liberalization measures covering 23 core areas, as the Caribbean nation tries to rescue a moribund economy that’s being squeezed by US sanctions.

item.title: Prestige Hospitality Is Said to Explore Stake Sale Instead of IPO
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd. is considering putting an initial public offering of its hospitality unit on hold and is instead exploring a minority stake sale to private equity investors, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Trafigura Targets Gupta Dubai Assets In Bid to Recoup Lost Nickel Millions
item.description: Trafigura Group asked a Dubai court to tighten financial restrictions on Prateek Gupta’s wife, as it looks to recoup more than $700 million in damages owed by him for sales of fake nickel.

item.title: Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Services Face Tough EU Antitrust Law
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s Azure and Amazon Web Services are on a collision course with the European Union’s tough digital competition rulebook, after an initial probe into their power over the cloud.

item.title: Men Jailed in UK for China Spying Targeting Pro-Democracy Groups
item.description: Two men were jailed for as long as 10 years after being found guilty of spying for China that targeted UK based pro-democracy campaigners.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Edge Down in Sign of Job Market Resilience
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week in a sign that the labor market remains in a “low-fire” mode.

item.title: Saudi, Qatar Add District Cooling Assets to Gulf Deals Pipeline
item.description: Two district cooling transactions linked to sovereign wealth funds in Saudi Arabia and Qatar are advancing, signaling continued investor appetite for Middle Eastern infrastructure assets despite geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Yardeni Says Fed May Hike If Serious About 2% Inflation Target
item.description: The Federal Reserve could well raise interest rates if it’s serious about getting inflation back down to its 2% target, according to veteran market strategist Ed Yardeni.

item.title: Chile Earns Outlier Status as Central Bank Fuels Rate-Cut Bets
item.description: Chile’s central bank dropped its hawkish bias this week, boosting bets on a rate cut within a year and leaving the nation as an outlier in a region where policymakers are signaling longer holds, and even hikes.

item.title: Hormuz Is Open But Shipowners Still Have Questions About Safety
item.description: Oil flows through the waterway are surging, but some doubts remain.

item.title: Chemicals Maker Ashland Pushed to Sell by Activist Cruiser
item.description: Cruiser Capital Advisors is pushing Ashland Inc. to sell itself, increasing the pressure on the specialty chemicals company after another activist investor called for a sale earlier this month.

item.title: Accenture Forecasts Lower Revenue on AI Upheaval, Mideast War
item.description: Accenture Plc said it’s expecting to reel in less revenue in the coming months, as artificial intelligence upends the consulting services industry and clients paused business due to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 18, 2026
item.description: US stocks are eyeing a rebound after President Donald Trump signed an interim peace deal with Iran. S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.5% at 7:45 a.m. in New York.

item.title: RBI Extends HDFC Bank Chairman Mistry’s Term by Three Months
item.description: India’s central bank approved the re-appointment of Keki Mistry as part-time chairman of HDFC Bank Ltd., months after the abrupt resignation of his predecessor sparked questions about governance issues at India’s largest private-sector lender.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise With Intel Leading Semiconductor Rally
item.description: US stock index futures climbed on Thursday as chips stocks extended Wednesday’s rally. Intel Corp. surged after President Donald Trump said the chipmaker struck a deal with Apple Inc. to design and produce semiconductors domestically.

item.title: South Africa Impeachment Panel to Oppose President’s Interdict
item.description: A panel of South African lawmakers that is conducting an inquiry into whether President Cyril Ramaphosa should be impeached over his handling of a robbery at his game farm said it will oppose his application to halt their work.

item.title: AI Power Boom Catapults NextEra to Top of Hybrid Debt Market
item.description: As utilities splash out on spending to power America’s AI boom, NextEra Energy Inc. has just emerged as the largest issuer of hybrid bonds worldwide.

item.title: Wellington’s Geary Says Stocks Rotation Trade Is Coming Back
item.description: The end of the Iran war is reviving investor appetite for stocks outside the US, according to Scott Geary, the head of global wealth at Wellington Management.

item.title: South African Floods Inflict Record $548 Million Damage on Cape
item.description: Recent flooding in South Africa’s Western Cape province, which includes the tourist hub of Cape Town, caused more than 9 billion rand ($548 million) of damage, a total that’s expected to rise once final assessments are completed.

item.title: Brazil Police Target Lula Ally in Banco Master Probe
item.description: Brazil’s federal police targeted Senator Jaques Wagner, the government leader in the upper house, as part of an investigation into alleged irregularities linked to Banco Master, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Warsh Establishes Inflation Credibility Now, Sets Up Fed Regime Change Later
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh vowed to restore price stability after leaving rates unchanged.

item.title: Lawyer Takes Fight to Keep Kids Off Social Media From Courthouse to Political Arena
item.description: Matthew Bergman pitched UK officials to protect kids from social media harms

item.title: Bank of England Holds Rates in 7-2 Vote as Oil Outlook Improves
item.description: The Bank of England held interest rates at 3.75% as it said the recent fall in oil prices was “encouraging,” even while two policymakers voted for an immediate quarter-point hike over concerns of persistent inflation.

item.title: Big Tech’s AI Spending Spree Is Making Stock Buybacks Disappear
item.description: The artificial-intelligence race is becoming so expensive that it’s snuffing out one of the key forces that has helped keep Big Tech stocks soaring for years: steady share buybacks.

item.title: Ships Race to Scrub Barnacles After Months Stuck in Persian Gulf
item.description: Vessels are getting scrubbed as they prepare to starting hauling cargo again

item.title: Europe Scales Down AI Ambitions With Smaller Data Center Tender
item.description: The European Union downsized plans for data centers to support artificial intelligence, outlining a call for tenders that includes smaller-scale facilities than originally envisioned.

item.title: US Treasuries Rebound After Warsh’s Debut at Fed Stoked Selloff
item.description: US Treasuries recovered from a selloff after the new Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh positioned himself as a credible figure to fight inflation.

item.title: Indonesia Taps Insider to Revive World’s Worst Stock Market
item.description: Indonesia named capital markets veteran Jeffrey Hendrik as chief executive of its stock exchange, the latest effort to bolster investor confidence in the world’s worst-performing equity market.
